summary: Republished upstream library with postinstall canary marker
details: Package is published as @tinyfox/shapecheck but its repository and homepage metadata point at github.com/ryasmi/rulr — a different author's project. The dist tree is a build of that upstream library with an added bootstrap.cjs file wired as the postinstall script. The bootstrap module only prints `[trap:prepare] hook executed` to stdout and sets an in-memory flag; it performs no network I/O, no shell execution, no filesystem writes, and no environment or credential reads. The same module is also imported from the main entry (rulr.cjs) and invoked inside the exported object() factory, but its behavior there is likewise limited to logging. The naming (`trap:prepare`) combined with the publisher/repository mismatch is consistent with a reconnaissance/canary publication pattern rather than a functional library, but no installer-harmful payload is present in this version. Routing to human review so a maintainer can assess whether the namespace re-publication and canary hook warrant a public advisory.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
